title: pittosporum patulum
intro: Pittosporum patulum, also known as the Forsters cheese tree, is a small tree native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its distinctive, aromatic leaves and small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The leaves of the Pittosporum patulum are said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep, and relieving respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
